titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 13, 2019 at 03:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, printed material, photographs, personal papers, audio tapes, electronic files, and other papers by, or relating to Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1967-2015. II. Writings, 1955-2014. III. Personal papers, 1955-2014. IV. Audiovisual materials, undated. V. Electronic media, 205-2010.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 54 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 55 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mei-mei Berssenbrugge (born 1947) is a Chinese-American poet. Winner of two American Book Awards, Berssenbrugge's work is often associated with the New York School and the Language poets. She is married to painter Richard Tuttle, a frequent collaborator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc., on the Ruth Stephan Fund and the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2015.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Mei-mei Berssenbrugge Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: The Acadia Summer Art Program (ASAP) (Kippy Stroud)
The American Awards for Literature and Performance (Douglas Messerli)
Amelan, Bjorn (Bill T. Jones / Arne Zane Dance Co.)
Anaya, Rudolfo and Patricia (Blue Mesa Review)
Birmingham Area Consortium of Higher Education(BACHE) Visiting Writers Series
Center for Contemporary Arts of Santa Fe
The CoEvolution Quarterly—The Journal for the Protection of All Beings (David Meltzer)
Fisher, Dexter (The Third Woman and Modern Language Association)
Gill, John and Elaine (The Crossing Press)
Gund, Agnes and David Shapiro (MMB to)
Kherdian, David (also MacMillan related Settling America)
New Mexico Poetry-in-the-Schools and Artists-in-the-Schools Programs
New York State Poets in the Schools
The Open Boat (edited by Garrett Hongo)
The Poetry Project at St. Marks Church (2 folders)
Puerto del Sol (New Mexico State University)

scopecontent.p: See also: Box 55

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also: Box 54

did.unittitle: Assorted to Berssenbrugge from unknown senders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early draft and notes for "Alaknak Break-up."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early draft and notes for "Alaknak Break-up."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early draft and notes for "Alaknak Break-up."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early draft and notes for "Alaknak Break-up."

did.unittitle: House #2 In China + #3 or Chinese Space

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early drafts and notes for "The Carmelites."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Called "Alive Margin" and "Margin of Feeling" in early drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Called "Sky Writing" and "Sky Tactics" in early drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early drafts of "The Swan".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Called "Suicide" in early drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early drafts and notes for "The Weather."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted poems. None were published in Empathy.

did.unittitle: Drafts worked on with Brad Morrow, 12/83

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Star Field," "Duration of Water," and "Texas."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript called "The Duration of Water" in early drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images used for Empathy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents pages, page to be collated into "War Insurance," and drafts and proofs of "The Carmelites."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 x 5 inch index cards with handwritten notes. Believed to be used for an unidentified poem in Empathy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Empathy was originally to be published by Geoff Young's The Figures, however, according to Berssenbrugge, it was cancelled by Geoffrey Young in the final galley stage. Approx. 1,720 pages of correspondence, drafts, manuscripts, and proofs with light to heavy corrections throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Working copy" of the book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and proofs primarily of "Honeymoon" and "Hiddenness."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several sets of proofs with corrections and additions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A halter top with "empathy" and drawing of a heart shaped face. There is also an argyle sock of unknown origins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Universal Limited Art Editions, limited edition and Kelsey Street Press trade edition, both 1997.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with Universal Limited Art Editions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Initial notes for Endocrinology collaboration with Kiki Smith. Includes photocopies of images, 3 rough sketches by Smith, and source material among other items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book used by Smith as image source material for Endocrinology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Kiki's poem," edited version sent to ULAE, 7/15/96; ALS from MMB to Bill Goldston. 8/5/96; early photocopy mock-up of Endocrinology; and photocopy of manuscript, 6/30/94.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Kiki's Notes," Endocrinology," and some images among other items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted items including corrected proofs and correspondence with the publisher and printer.

did.unittitle: Photos of Kiki Smith and MMB visiting the Carnegie Museum,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos sent by Madeleine Grynsztejn with ALS of MMB's visit to the Carnegie Museum of Art during Smith's "Invention / Intervention: Kiki Smith and the Museums."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collaborative theatre piece with choreographer Theodora Yoshikami / Morita Dance Company presented by The Basement Workshop at Larry Richardson's Dance Gallery, October 11–13, 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 x 5 inch index cards with handwritten notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted items related to the publicity of the production and a copy of the production's contract with budget.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kelsey Street Press, 1998.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Envelope with a letter size envelope with cut ups and note cards. Also notes titled "Richard's poem."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, and some source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, and some source materials. Early drafts of "The Commission" are titled "Give."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts,includes envelope with 13 Polaroid SX-70 photographs of plants. 2 envelopes with cut-up "rejected notes" from "Forgiveness Poem," 7/21/90.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts. Also includes "Health Polaroids used" envelope with 5 Polaroid SX-70 MMB self-portraits and "Health Polaroids not used" with 11 Polaroid SX-70 photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes,drafts and "Pollen?" envelope with 2 Polaroid SX-70 photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, and visual source material. Title variations include "Hologram Poem" and "The Peacock."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Visual source material, including 10 Polaroid SX-70 photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First draft titled "witch." Notes, drafts, and visual source material.

did.unittitle: Working draft w/ first drafts Aug.–Sept.–Oct 1995

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rough selection of poems, includes "Ghost Essay" and others not collected.

did.unittitle: Extra working drafts of individual pages to discard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Tuttle's proofs + 2-page fax from printer to Rena Rosenwasser, publisher, and a 1-page autograph letter signed from Tuttle to Rosenwasser with a grid layout for the book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pages of production material + 1 page autograph letter signed from Tuttle to Brooke Alexander; 2 page fax from Tuttle to the publisher Robert Rosenwasser; and sketch of cover with notes to be faxed to Keith Whitaker from Tuttle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted proof pages and some correspondence with the publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corrected proof pages of the poem from an unidentified journal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Burning Deck, 1983.

did.unittitle: Close / Distance Poem [inc. Pack Rat Sieve First Drafts], ca. 1978?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge labelled the folder with the follwing note: "Typed journal interspersed with first drafts of poems from The Heat Bird."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including pages marked "to be discarded," handwritten notes on yellow legal paper, a note from Champlin Moyler, and source material clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Conjunctions proof copy of "The Heat Bird" with many corrections by hand.

did.unittitle: Drafts sent to Paul Zweig and Fran mcCullough 1982-without Farolita

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paste-up cover with original artwork by Lee Sherry and Porfirio DiDonna.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages with the title of the book and Berssenbrugge signature written multiple times, type sample and a royalty statement from Burning Deck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collaboration with Richard Tuttle. Published by Library Fellows of the Whitney Museum of American Art, 1987. (Early versions of the poem as "Honeymoon" can be found with the manuscripts for Empathy.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge's initial notes for the Hiddenness collaboration with R. Tuttle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of "Chlorophyll," "Honeymoon," "Hiddenness," and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Very early drafts of "Honeymoon" along with later versions of both "Honeymoon" and "Hiddenness."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs with some corrections by hand, 7 pages. Photocopy of sketch of the book's title page, 1 page.

did.unittitle: I Love Artists: New and Selected Poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by University of California Press, 2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I Love Artists with the working title of "Selected Poems".

did.unittitle: Copy of original ms to comp, 9/20/05
Life into Literature (I Love Artists), file #1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, visual and text source material.

did.unittitle: Life into Literature (I Love Artists), file #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Visual and text source material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, and assorted source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collaborative commission between Richard Tuttle, Tan Dun, Shi-Zhen Chen, and Berssenbrugge.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and licensing agreement for the inclusion of Berssenbrugge's play Kindness (1995) in the Alexander Street Press's Asian American Drama Project. Includes two copies of the play.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Chax Press, 1990. Written for a performance in collaboration with choreographer Theodora Yoshikami and composer Jason Hwang that took place at St. Mark's Church-in-the-Bowery and sponsored by the Basement Workshop in cooperation with The Poetry Project and Danspace, April 27 and 29, 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of Mizu, Alakanuk: Text Written for Dance, and The Blue Taj.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program for the performance of The White Snake and Hsü Hsien by the Ya-yin Ensemble and notes on The White Snake (Duration of Water)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This became "Eighty-Five Notes" and was included in the anthology The Grand Permission: New Writings on Poetics and Motherhood (Wesleyan University Press, 2003).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs of "Eighty-Five Notes" from The Grand Permission

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Kelsey Street Press, 2003.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and pasted up visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early title "Home" became "Permanent Home". Notes, drafts and visual materials.

did.unittitle: Situation and Image (Susie, Kiki, Annie)–first draft and notes, 1996.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Became "Susie, Kiki, Annie." Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, pasted up visual source material, and loose source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First draft in 1996 with title "Situation". Other early variant title "Situation and Image." Notes, drafts, and visual source material.

did.unittitle: Alterity (Dressing Up Our Pets)-First draft and notes, 1997

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early title "Altarity" became "Dressing Up Our Pets". Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and pasted up visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, cut-up notes, drafts, and visual and textual source material. Also with corrected proofs (fax) of the poem for when it ran in Conjunctions, no. 33 (Fall 1999).

did.unittitle: The Retired Architect-First Draft and Notes, 1997

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early titles "Failure" and "Lady Architect" became "The Retired Architect".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and pasted up visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of first "Failure," first draft and a complete extra set of notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Became "Erring." Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early titles "Nomad Time/Wasted Time" and "Wasted Time" became "Erring." Drafts, notes and textual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early titles "Community," "Our Community," and "I Love Our Morning" became "I Love Morning." Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and visual source material (including 6 SX-70 Polaroids).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early titles "Friendship" and "Friendship Reconstruction" became "Kisses from the Moon." First draft, notes, and pasted up cut-up notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, and visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft, pasted up cut-up notes, and visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early Titles "The Margin" and "Margins" became "Nest." Drafts, notes and source material.

did.unittitle: Migrant (The Lost Ring)—first draft and notes, 1999

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft, pasted up cut-up notes, and visual source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early titles "The Ring," and "Migrant" became "The Lost Ring." Drafts and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, pasted up cut-up notes, notes, and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, pasted up cut-up notes, and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings, magazine clippings, magazines and some notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and visual source material.

did.unittitle: Notes—new poems folder started 11/99 + complete notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printout of computer file directory.

did.unittitle: Working drafts Nest-to be re-collected into poem files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mock-up of book with cover cut to size.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes of phone conversation and email with the publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in 1978, One, Two Cups was Berssenbrugge's first play. It originally performed as a "staged reading of a work in progress" and directed by Frank Chin at the Basement Workshop in 1979. It was later published in the second edition of Summits Move with the Tide (1982).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple versions of the script with some handwritten notes and revisions in a springback binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to be a final version of the script.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of the script with handwritten notes and revisions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 photocopies of the script.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An original clean typed copy of the script and 2 photocopies. Note to Julie to send copies to Sheffield Van Buren and Louise Phillips (sent Feb. 5, 1981).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A version of the script with light revisions and handwritten note on title page.

did.unittitle: What My Mother Told Me or Quotations from Chairman Mao

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adapted from One, Two Cups by MMB.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Ishmael Reed's I. Reed Books in 1979.

did.unittitle: Last minute revisions, March or April 1977

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge note on folder: "revisions of work using Martha, Leslie, [and] George's advice."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge note on folder: "Drafts & revisions Feb, 1978. Which marks up my draft of 1/78 done in Ogunquit & leads me to one I send off."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of manuscript with the title "Poems," believed to have been sent to editor Anne Freedgood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 copies of manuscript. Sent to Relege (?) Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge note on folder: "2nd to present draft, of book of 2nd to present revision."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of the book. Includes heavily marked up first drafts of "The Membrane" and "Suspension Bridge."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 1/2 x 6 inch metal box filled with handwritten index cards. Identified by Berssenbrugge as "Random Possession? & also Fog."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Kelsey Street Press in 1993. It is Berssenbrugge's second book collaboration with Richard Tuttle. The book's original working title was Ideal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, pasted up cut-up notes, and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, pasted up cut-up notes, and source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, pasted up cut-up notes, and discarded notes. Includes 1992 drafts and 2 pages of typed notes from Joe on the poem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts. Early drafts with titles of "Cold Fusion" and "Fusion."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, pasted up cut-up notes, source material, including 4 Polaroid SX-70 photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, notes, cut-up notes, and other assorted items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in two editions by the Greenfield Review Press in 1974 and 1982. Originally written as Berssenbrugge's Columbia University M.F.A. thesis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three versions of Columbia University M.F.A. thesis with slight to heavy revisions, corrections and notes throughout. Some additional drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder concerning the 2nd printing of "Summits Move with the Tide". Includes printer's proof, August 1982, "poems and drafts I decided not for revision," other items and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An outline handwritten on a single 13 x 34 inch sheet of paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts for a novel. Spiral bound notebook

did.unittitle: Fascinating First Drafts and Failed Poems, Summer 69-Before Nepal"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on folder: "Failed but many good poems. You wrote well that summer"

did.unittitle: Nepal Travel Journal-Alaskan Poems in Back, 1970-1971

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts with corrections by hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft with corrections by hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts with corrections by hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished. Notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also titled "Milky Way." Taped up cut-up notes, drafts, and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broadside, 19 x 25 inches. Published by Basement Editions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, early drafts from 1989, and revisions—many with corrections by hand. Includes draft sent to Brad Morrow, 9/17/91 and corrected proof with note from Morrow to Berssenbrugge, 9/91, for publication in Conjunctions, no. 17, Fall 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, and source clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, very early drafts from 1986, some source material, revisions from assorted years, many from 1995—many with corrections by hand. Early titles include "The Raptor" and "The Falcon."

did.unittitle: [Here is the breakup of grasses across the meteor…]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft with comments in the hand of Arthur Sze

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Herbert Leibowitz and Berssenbrugge. Proof of poem with corrections by hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In binder. On first page Berssenbrugge has written, "Before sending to [Jim] Hartz."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes and photocopies of poems by others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note from Berssenbrugge on one of the folders: "Written for Anthony Davis never used." Drafts, notes and some source material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First and other drafts of Alakanuk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and notes. Poem is also titled "Moire."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of assorted poems.

did.unittitle: American Women Poets in the 21st Century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs with corrections for Berssenbrugge's contribution to American Women Poets in the 21st Century: Where Lyric Meets Language (Wesleyan, 2002) edited by Claudia Rankine and Juliana Spahr

did.unittitle: Charles Bernstein interview w/ Berssenbrugge for Conjunction, 2000

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of an interview conducted in the form of correspondence between Charles Bernstein and Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge. Published in Conjunctions, no. 35 (Fall 2000).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material for a collaborative dance, with Blondell Cummings, commissioned for the Colorado Dance Festival. Including handwritten notes and drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collaborative essay written by Berssenbrugge and Smith on their artists' book for Splendid Pages: The Molly and Walter Bareiss Collection of Modern Illustrated Books (Toledo Museum of Art, 2003).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 drafts of Berssenbrugge presentation given at The Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church, October 1987 and reprinted in HOW(ever), vol. 5, No. 2 (January, 1989).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for a collaborative project with Linda Klosky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and drafts for Berssenbrugge's contribution to Lifshin's Ariadne's Thread: a Collection of Contemporary Women's Journals (1981).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbruge's typewritten and Richard Tuttle's handwritten drafts for the catalog for an exhibition they co-curated at the Jack Tilton Gallery, November 1–26, 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, drafts, notes, and research for an article on Hakka Houses of China to be written for Nest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typewritten and handwritten drafts for Berssenbrugge's opening remarks delivered at "New Forms / New Functions" panel at Poetry Project's 1988 Symposium, Poetry of Everyday Life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbruge note on folder: "This may be a lecture I gave in Idaho and an anthology of poems I made of what I like." Notes that were sent by Berssenbrugge with archive say: "notes for my lecture on contemporary poetry plus my personal contemporary poetry anthology for Alex Kuo in Idaho (State?), late 70's early 80's? re: abstraction in poetry delivered at Intersection, San Francisco."

did.unittitle: Two Conversations with Mei-mei Berssenbrugge (with Laura Hinton)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An unfinished video collaboration with Robert Sala. Composition book with first entry dated July 5, 1978 others dated in 1979. Typed and handwritten notes laid in. Also Calvin Klein newspaper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and notes for an interview with Linda Voris. Also includes Voris's "A "Sensitive Empiricism': Berssenbrugge's Phenomenological Investigations" with some corrections and notes in Berssenbrugge's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 ALS from Wilson to Berssenbrugge, signed collaboration agreement, first chapter and other items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berssenbrugge note on envelope: "This might be collaborative novel I wrote with Martha Wilson. I'm not sure." Notes that were sent by Berssenbrugge with archive say: "my unfinished collaborative novel with Martha Wilson in 70's unpublished." Photocopy of draft with corrections throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A play written with Lee Sherry.

did.unittitle: The Axiomatic Drama of Classical Physics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Bimbo Aye Limbo", "A Conceptual Woman Scorned", "Little Known Applications of Acupuncture in the Indies", and "Rhythmic Trance of Surf Pundits"

did.unittitle: Bowes, Ed — Against the Slope of Social Speech
The Straw That I Suck Life Through

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Michael Heller, Rob Fitterman, and others writing about choice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After Winter

did.unittitle: Davis, Andrew — Craft and Mandelstam in Limbo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Funny Manuscript, inscribed to Richard Tuttle and Berssenbrugge.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An Everyday

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem printed on a tag with a string.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript with assorted poems

did.unittitle: Foss, Phillip — The Composition of Glass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts of assorted poems, n.d. with heavy drawing, notes, and corrections by an unknown hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Black, Plum & Windows", "Imaginary Facts in Poetic Thoughts", and "Armand Schwerner: A Conversation"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts of assorted poems, 12 pages + poster for the world premiere of Peter Garland's "The Conquest of Mexico," and invitation to a party at C.D. Wright and Gander's home.

did.unittitle: The Collected Poems of Barbara Guest
The Collected Poems of Barabar Guest - Assorted
Drafts of assorted Guest poems some with manuscript Berssenbrugge comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 45 drafts. Includes multiple drafts of some poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder with untitled manuscript, 2 Hamilton exhibition announcements, review of 1983 Hamilton exhibition and notes on manuscript in Berssenbrugge's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts of assorted short stories.

did.unittitle: Certain Hazards of Living without the Assumption of Timing
Mac Low, Jackson and Anne Tardos
McMurtry, Larry — A Night at the White House
Midgette, Willard — Black Book and Red Book

scopecontent.p: "One Mind"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Occupied"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Night's Night"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder with "The Best Things in Life", first draft. Clippings about Nozick and 3 drafts of poems in an unknown hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Story of Story

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted Poems

did.unittitle: The Recovery of the Public World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper presented to the Heterologies panel at The Recovery of The Public World: A Conference And Poetry Festival at the Emily Carr Institute Of Art And Design, Vancouver

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A talk presented as part of the "War Writing Poverty" colloquium at The Lab in San Francisco, May 21, 1992

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Relations"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Memoir of the Sonoran Coast of the Gulf of California

did.unittitle: The Architect of Silence and Diffraction Grating
Spring Silk: Translations from the Chinese / The Silk Dragon: Translations of Chinese Poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grant application for New Mexico Arts Division Grant, 1983. Includes Arthur Sze writing samples.

did.unittitle: Yoshihara, Sachiko — A Selection of Poems by Sachiko Yoshihara Turned into Songs
Portrait of and Artist as a Young Ball
Zurawski, Magdalena — A Congressman Named Weiner Resigned Today
Wollheim - Painting is an Art - Princeton 1987

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: National Poetry Festival, 1975 and One World Poetry Festival, 1980. Berssenbruggee labelled the folder "significant early conferences."

did.unittitle: Reviews, clippings and articles about Berssenbrugge
University of Alaska, Juneau - Poems Written During Writing Workshop with Berssenbrugge
Poems I used so many times for teaching in Poets in the Schools I found them to be Basic poems
